title: One of Us Is Going to Hell
author: Benjamin G Nechicattu
description: A lighthearted yet honest reflection on growing up surrounded by different religions — and the strange statistical conclusion that follows.
content:
*I'm confused.*

I was born and brought up in a **Pentecostal** family.
Some of my relatives are **Roman Catholic**.
A few are **Mar Thoma**.

My friends?
Some are **Hindus**.
Some are **Muslims**.

And every one of them believes strongly that **their path is the right one**.

---

Recently, I started thinking about something strange.

*What if there really is an entity called God?*

If God exists, then logically there must be something like **Heaven** and **Hell**.
That's what most religions say.

But then a strange thought follows.

*If all these religions claim to be right…*
*then statistically speaking…*
***most of us are probably wrong.***

Which means, according to someone's belief system,
**one of us is going to Hell.**

Maybe more than one.

Maybe almost everyone.

---

And then I thought of something even funnier.

If simply **questioning things like this** is considered sinful somewhere…
then congratulations.

*I'm probably already booked for Hell.*

Which means many of the people I know —
*good people, kind people, honest people* —
might also end up there.

---

So if that's the case…

I guess I'll see many familiar faces.

**See you in Hell. 😉**
